bio.title: PROFILE
bio.subtitle: A creator of musical projects
bio.text: Cristian Basto is an Argentine double bassist, composer, arranger, and conductor based in Barcelona. His work brings together performance, composition, arranging, conducting, and teaching in an artistic approach that takes Argentine music as its point of departure while engaging in dialogue with contemporary creation.
bio.text-extra: Rather than building a career centered on a single role, his artistic practice is shaped through projects in which creation and performance are part of the same creative process. Deeply rooted in the Argentine musical tradition, he explores new ways of connecting with jazz, concert music, and other musical expressions from Latin America.

text1.text-p1:
He is a member of the Astor Piazzolla Quintet, the ensemble that continues the legacy of one of the most influential bodies of work in Argentine music and tours throughout Europe and the Americas. At the same time, he performs with the Orquestra Assintomática (Lisbon), an international artistic collective that brings together musicians from diverse musical traditions.

Within this context, he has collaborated with artists such as António Zambujo, Salvador Sobral, Maria João, Cristina Branco, Mario Laginha, Yamandu Costa, Guinga, and Aaron Parks, expanding his work into new musical languages and artistic scenes.

Throughout his career, he has also shared the stage and creative projects with renowned artists including Susana Rinaldi, Amelita Baltar, Diego Schissi, Julieta Laso, Noelia Sinkunas, Chango Spasiuk, Andrea Motis, Luis Alberto Spinetta, Los Fabulosos Cadillacs, and YSY A, building a career defined by the dialogue between tango, folk music, jazz, rock, and contemporary popular music.

text2.text-p2:
Creation lies at the heart of his artistic work.

He is the founder and artistic director of Cuarteto Insurgente, a Barcelona-based string ensemble dedicated to exploring new possibilities for contemporary Argentine music, and leads Basto Ensamble, the platform through which he develops his original compositions, arrangements, and productions.

Alongside these projects, he maintains an active career as an ensemble director and educator in Argentina, Spain, and Portugal, viewing music education as a natural extension of his artistic practice.

text3.text-p3:
His music has been performed in some of the leading concert halls across Europe and the Americas, including Carnegie Hall (New York), Concertgebouw (Amsterdam), Philharmonie Berlin (Berlin), Wiener Konzerthaus (Vienna), Kennedy Center (Washington, D.C.), National Arts Centre (Ottawa), Teatro Colón (Buenos Aires), Maison symphonique – Place des Arts (Montreal), and La Seine Musicale (Paris).

He has also appeared at international festivals including Festival International de Jazz de Montréal, Jazzaldia San Sebastián, La Villette, Roskilde Festival, Lollapalooza Argentina, Expo 2020 Dubai, and the Buenos Aires International Tango Festival.

text4.text-p4: His work as a composer, arranger, and performer has received two Carlos Awards, two Latin Grammy nominations, an Honorary Mention at the SONUS International Music Festival, and support from the Mecenazgo program of the Ministry of Culture of the City of Buenos Aires for the creation of Suite Villa Urquiza.

contact.contact-p: To communicate with Cristian, write to cristianbasto79@gmail.com

classes.class-p: I share my passion for music through personalized classes of double bass, composition, arrangements and orchestration, adapted for musicians of different levels. I also conduct double bass and typical orchestra workshops, where we explore the techniques and characteristic gestures of those great orchestras. In production, I work as a team with the artists, from the creation of arrangements to the direction of recordings and my participation as a double bassist, always seeking an authentic result.
classes.class-p2: The main idea of ​​Level I of this workshop is to provide effective tools to approach tango, understand the essential function of the double bass within the genre and encourage the search for each musician's own and distinctive musicality. The focus will be on the repertoire of the most influential typical orchestras that marked the golden era of tango in the 1940s and 1950s, such as those of Aníbal Troilo, Osvaldo Pugliese, Horacio Salgán and Juan D'Arienzo. These orchestras not only defined the style and sound of tango, but also consolidated two fundamental accompaniment models that form the backbone of the orchestra both in harmonic terms as rhythmic: Marcato and syncopation.
classes.title-class2: Tango Workshop: Typical Orchestras of the 40s and 50s
classes.class-p3: Cristian Basto has worked as a teacher directing typical ensembles and orchestras at the National Academy of Tango, at the Center for the Development of Tango and Rioplatense Culture (CDTCR) “Escuela de Tango Orlando Goñi”, and at “La Academia Tango Club”.If you want to learn to play tango, this workshop is focused on the typical tango orchestras of the 40s and 50s, considered the golden age of tango. It is aimed at both professional and amateur musicians as well as anyone interested, regardless of the instrument they play, in learning the techniques and stylistic resources that shaped the unmistakable sound of these emblematic orchestras. During the workshop, we will work on the gestures and actions necessary to recreate the characteristic sound of these orchestras, capturing their rhythmic, melodic and stylistic essence.

sheet-music.sheet-music-p: Here I share a selection of scores that range from tango and Argentine popular music to contemporary compositions and arrangements of different genres. Each piece reflects my interest in creating versatile and accessible music, without neglecting depth and quality .
sheet-music.sheet-music-p1: My compositions explore innovative sounds and textures, influenced by contemporary music and current technologies. Each work is an invitation to discover the unexpected within the familiar. In my arrangements, the focus is to transform and reinventing known melodies, adapting them to explore new musical perspectives I have collaborated on albums such as Pasa by Limón García, A Berisso by Noelia Sinkunas, ¿Y el fin del amor? Laso, among others, providing a distinctive touch to each piece. In this section, you will find arrangements designed for various groups and styles, such as orchestras, ensembles and quartets. If you have a musical project or group and are looking for original arrangements or compositions, you can count on me. to give a unique and professional twist to each performance.
sheet-music.title-sheet3: Suite for string ensemble, bandoneon, cello and piano
sheet-music.title-sheet4: Dance for piano and double bass
sheet-music.title-sheet5: Works for string quintet and piano

donations.donations-p: You can support my content by making a small monthly or one-time contribution, to be able to make more and better content.
